name: Coconut Chocolate Candy
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, melt the butter over low heat and do not allow it to brown.
2. Whisk in the condensed milk, vanilla, and salt.
3. Add the powdered sugar, coconut, and pecans. Mix quickly; use your hands if necessary.
4. Coat a 9 x 13-inch baking pan with vegetable spray.
5. Transfer the candy mixture to the pan and press firmly into place.
6. Cover and store in the coldest part of your refrigerator overnight.
7. In a double boiler set over hot (not boiling) water, melt the paraffin and chocolate chips.
8. Cut the cooled coconut candy into 1-inch squares.
9. Using a toothpick, dip each square into the melted chocolate to coat.
10. Place the coated candies on wax paper to harden. They may need to be refrigerated until completely hardened and can then be stored in the refrigerator.
